description_long: This is a military-style utility helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in matte olive green. It features a large main rotor with long, straight black blades and a smaller tail rotor at the end of a narrow tail boom. The cockpit is equipped with multiple rectangular windows, providing wide visibility for the pilots, and the side doors are open, revealing a simple interior with bench seating. The helicopter rests on two black metal landing skids that run parallel beneath the fuselage, connected by crossbars for stability. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative markings and a focus on function over form. The asset is reminiscent of the iconic Bell UH-1 'Huey' helicopter, commonly seen in military and rescue operations.
description: A matte olive green military-style helicopter with a large main rotor, open side doors, and black landing skids. It has a streamlined fuselage and multiple cockpit windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the open side door, cockpit windows, and the full length of the main and tail rotors.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow profile, cockpit windows, and the alignment of the main rotor above.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with open door, visible seating inside, and clear view of the tail boom and rotors.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail boom, tail rotor, and the back of the cockpit windows.
attribute_rotor_blades: The main rotor has two long, straight black blades made of composite or metal, with no visible markings or color variations. The blades are thin and extend well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: The tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, featuring two short black blades arranged perpendicularly to the tail boom. No protective shroud or markings are visible.
attribute_landing_skids: The helicopter has two parallel black metal landing skids with a simple tubular shape, connected by crossbars for stability. The skids are slightly curved at the ends and appear robust.
attribute_cockpit_windows: There are multiple rectangular cockpit windows at the front and sides, framed in black, with a clear or lightly tinted appearance. The windows are large, providing wide visibility for the pilots.
